title: Automatic event collection
story: As a developer using iSDLC, I want the framework to automatically capture usage events (workflow starts, completions, gate results, verb invocations) so that I can understand my development patterns without manual effort.
acceptance_criteria: Events are emitted automatically during normal framework use with no developer action required
Events capture workflow type, verb, phase, timing, and gate results
Event emission adds less than 5ms latency per hook execution
Events are stored locally regardless of telemetry consent state

title: Compaction frequency tracking
story: As a developer using iSDLC, I want to know how often my conversations compact so that I can understand context window pressure from the session cache.
acceptance_criteria: Session cache reloads are detected and recorded as events
First load is distinguished from subsequent reloads (compaction indicators)
Session cache size in bytes is captured on each load
/isdlc stats shows average compactions per session

title: Reliable local event storage
story: As a developer using iSDLC, I want my analytics events stored reliably on disk so that I never lose usage data even when offline or when the collector is unreachable.
acceptance_criteria: Events are appended to a local JSONL file immediately on emission
Store rotates when exceeding 1MB to prevent unbounded growth
Events persist across sessions and survive framework restarts
Store operations never block or fail framework operations

title: Batched telemetry transmission
story: As a framework author, I want opted-in installations to transmit anonymized usage data in batches so that I can understand adoption patterns across all users.
acceptance_criteria: Events are transmitted in batches, not individually
Transmission retries after 10 minutes if the collector is unreachable
Successfully transmitted events are flushed from the local store
Transmission only occurs when telemetry is enabled

title: Collector service receives telemetry
story: As a framework author, I want a collector endpoint that receives and stores anonymized telemetry batches so that I can query aggregate usage data across all iSDLC installations.
acceptance_criteria: Collector accepts HTTP POST with JSON batch payload
Collector validates schema and rejects malformed or oversized batches
Stored events are queryable by framework version, event type, and time range
Collector runs on free-tier PaaS with auto-deploy from the repo

title: One-time telemetry consent
story: As a developer using iSDLC, I want to be asked once whether to share anonymous usage data so that I maintain control over what leaves my machine.
acceptance_criteria: Consent prompt appears after first successful workflow completion
Prompt mentions the ability to preview what would be sent
Saying no means no transmission but local collection continues
Decision persists and is changeable via /isdlc telemetry on|off

title: Local usage stats dashboard
story: As a developer using iSDLC, I want to run /isdlc stats and see a summary of my framework usage so that I can identify my own friction points and efficiency patterns.
acceptance_criteria: Stats show workflow counts by type with completion rates and average duration
Stats show most-used verbs, tier selection distribution, and phase timing
Stats show friction breakdown (developer vs framework vs ambiguous)
Trend indicators appear when 7+ days of data exist

title: Telemetry preview before opting in
story: As a developer using iSDLC, I want to preview exactly what anonymized data would be transmitted so that I can make an informed decision about opting in.
acceptance_criteria: /isdlc telemetry preview shows anonymized versions of pending events
No field in the preview output contains PII (slugs, paths, project names, branch names)
Preview works regardless of current telemetry state (enabled, disabled, or unset)

title: PII-free anonymization guarantee
story: As a developer using iSDLC, I want assurance that transmitted telemetry contains zero personally identifiable information so that I can trust the system with my usage data.
acceptance_criteria: Anonymization uses an explicit allowlist -- new fields are excluded by default
Anonymous instance ID is a random UUID, never derived from machine or user identity
Fields like slug, file_path, branch_name, project_name, description are stripped entirely
failure_detail (which may contain file paths) is replaced with failure_category enum

title: Configurable data retention
story: As a developer using iSDLC, I want to control how long analytics data is retained locally so that I can balance history depth against disk usage.
acceptance_criteria: Retention is controlled by both max_entries and max_days (whichever triggers first)
Defaults are 10,000 entries and 90 days
Settings are configurable in .isdlc/config.yaml
Oldest events are pruned first
